source_metadata.title_raw: Gary L. Edwards - A-18-120 - July 11, 2018 - Sonoma

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Gary L. Edwards 
Sonoma City Councilmember 
No. 1 The Plaza 
Sonoma, CA 95476-6618 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-18-120 
Dear Mr. Edwards: 
July 11, 2018 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 Please note that we are only providing advice under the 
conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions 
such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090. Additionally, please note that we are not a 
finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we 
provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts 
underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for additional advice. 
QUESTION 
May you take part in City Council decisions related to the renovation and expansion of the 
Sonoma Cheese Factory (the "Cheese Factory") given that the Cheese Factory is within 891 feet of 
a property owned by your family trust? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. You may take part in the decisions related to the Cheese Factory renovation project 
because the project will not have a reasonably foreseeable financial effect on your financial 
interests. 
FACTS AS PROVIDED BY THE REQUESTOR 
The Property 
You are Sonoma City Councilmember. Your family trust has a 100-percent ownership 
interest in real property (the "Property") located in the City. The Property, a 700-square-foot 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-18-120 
Page No. 2 
building with one parking space designated solely for disabled persons, is currently leased to a 
mortgage firm under a five-year lease, with the option to extend the lease by an additional five 
years. Currently, the monthly lease payment is $2,450.00. You, your wife, and your children are the 
trustees and beneficiaries of the trust. 
Cheese Factory 
The Property is located approximately 891 feet away from the from the nearest property line 
of the Cheese Factory. The Cheese Factory property consists of two parcels on the north side of the 
Sonoma Plaza, mid-block on Spain Street. The two parcels, which create an L-shaped site, have a 
combined area of approximately of 20,335 square feet. While significant portions of the building 
are vacant or underutilized, the Cheese Factory building features a retail and restaurant space (with 
103 indoor seats), including a wine tasting component. 
· 
The Cheese Factory property has a General Plan land use designation of commercial. The 
commercial land use designation is intended to provide areas for retail, hotel, service, medical, and 
office development, in association with apartments and mixed-use developments and necessary 
public improvements. The Cheese Factory property is located within the Historic Overlay zone and 
the Plaza' Retail Overlay zone. 
The proposed project is intended to renovate, reconfigure, and expand the Cheese Factory 
into a multi-tenant marketplace featuring locally-soureed artisan foods, cheeses, baked goods, wine, 
coffee, and other related food and non-food products. Two restaurant tenant spaces are proposed 
and the Plaza-facing element of the Cheese Factory building, which has been found to be 
historically-significant, would be retained. New construction would enable architectural 
enhancements that would improve the visual compatibility of the rear portion of the Cheese Factory 
with adjoining historic buildings on the State Parks property. As approved by the Planning 
Commission, the project would result in a net increase in commercial space of 3,538 square feet. 
Although no on-site parking is proposed in conjunction with the building expansion 
associated with the proposed project, a nearby parking lot is currently configured to provide 
approximately 142 parking spaces, and is available for general public use. The City is currently in 
negotiations to re-implement a long-term lease of the parking lot that would enable the City to make 
improvements, including by expanding the lot's parking capacity. 
You noted that the proposed Cheese Factory project structures will not be visible from the 
Property. A traffic analysis found that the mitigated project would not have a significant impact on 
any Plaza intersection, including the intersection of First Street East/East Napa Street, which is the 
closest intersection to the Property. In addition, the analysis found that the mitigated project would 
not have a significant impact on parking demand in the Plaza. 
ANALYSIS 
. 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. 
A conflict of interest may arise only when it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a 
material financial effect on the official, the official's financial interests, or on the official's 


File No. A-18-120 
Page No. 3 
immediate family that is distinguishable from its effect on the public generally. (Section 87103.) 
Specific to your facts, Section 87103 interests from which a conflict of interest may arise include: 
• 
An interest in a business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment of 
$2,000 or more (Section 87103(a)); or in which the official is a director, officer, partner, 
trustee, employee, or holds any position of management (Section 87103(d)). 
• 
An interest in any source of income aggregating $500 or more to the official within 12 
months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(c).) 
• 
An interest in your personal finances and those of immediate family members. (See Section 
87103.) An official always has an interest in his or her personal finances. 2 
Additionally, Regulation 18234(a) provides that an official has an interest in the pro rata 
share of the interest in real property, sources of income, and investments of a trust in which the 
official has a direct, indirect, or beneficial interest of 10-percent or greater. An official has a direct, 
indirect, or beneficial interest in real property, sources of income, and investments of a trust if the 
official is: 
"(1) A trustor and: 
"(A) Can revoke or terminate the trust; 
"(B) Has retained or reserved any rights to the income or principal of the trust, or 
retained any reversionary or remainder interest; or 
"(C) Has retained or reserved any power of appointment, including but nqt limited to 
the power to change the trustee, or the power to amend, alter or designate, either 
alone or in conjunction with anyone else, the person or persons who shall possess or 
enjoy the trust property or income." (Regulation 18234(c).) 
You and your wife are the trustors of the trust and have reserved some power to change 
trustees and beneficiaries.3 The trust itself is operated as a commercial rental enterprise, which 
qualifies as a "business entity" under Section 82005, and you have an investment in the trust worth 
$2,000 or more. Therefore, you have an interest in the trust as a business entity. You also have an 
interest in the Property owned by the trust, and source of income interests from the mortgage firm 
and the trust, assuming your pro rata share of trust income aggregates to $500 more in the previous 
12 months. 
2 An effect on an official'; interest in his or her personal finances or those of immediate family members is not 
considered separately if the decision also affects the official's interest in a business entity or real property (Regulation 
18702.5(c)). Because the decisions at issue also affect your business and real property interests, we do not further 
analyze your personal financial interests or those of your immediate family. 
3 See Edwards Advice Letter, No. A-18-011. 


Foreseeability and Materiality 
File No. A-18-120 
Page No. 4 
The standard for foreseeability varies depending on whether an interest is explicitly 
involved in the decision.4 For a financial interest that is not explicitly involved in the decision at 
issue, the financial effect of the decision on an official's interest is reasonably foreseeable if it can 
be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical. (Regulation 
18701 (b ). ) Your interests are not explicitly involved in the decisions at issue. 
The Properly 
For real property not explicitly involved in a decision, Regulation 18702.2(a) establishes 
that a foreseeable effect of a governmental decision on an official's financial interest is material if 
the decision: 
"(10) Would change the character of the parcel ofreal property by 
substantially altering traffic levels or intensity of use, including parking, of property 
surrounding the official's real property parcel, the view, privacy, noise levels, or air 
quality, including odors, or any other factors that would affect the market value of 
the real property parcel in which the official has a financial interest; 
"(12) Would cause a reasonably prudent person, using due care and 
consideration under the circumstances, to believe that the governmental decision 
was of such a nature that its reasonably foreseeable effect would influence the 
market value of the official's property." 
Here, you indicated that the Cheese Factory project is located 891 feet from the Property, 
would not have significant impacts, as mitigated, on traffic or parking near the Property, and is not 
visible from the Property. Currently, the Cheese Factory is utilized as restaurant, retail, and wine 
tasting space, and the proposed project intends to utilize the space in a similar manner. Given the 
distance from the Property and the absence of significant impacts on the surrounding area, including 
the Property, it does not appear that City decisions related to the expansion and reconfiguration of 
the Cheese Factory will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on your property 
interest. 
4 An official's financial interest is explicitly involved in a governmental decision if the interest is a named 
party in, or subject of, the decision. A financial interest is the subject of a proceeding if the decision involves the 
issuance, renewal, approval, denial, or revocation of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the 
financial interest, or if the decision affects a real property interest described in Regulation l 8702.2(a)(l )-(6). 
(Regulation 18701(a).) 


Trust and Mortgage Finn 
File No. A-18-120 
Page No. 5 
Regulation 18702.1 sets forth the materiality standards applicable to a decision's effect on 
an official's interest in a business.5 Pertinent to your facts, Regulation 18702.l(b) provides that a 
decision's effect is material if a prudent person with sufficient information would find it reasonably 
foreseeable that the decision's effect would contribute to a change in the value of the privately-held 
business entity. 
In this case, the Property is currently leased to a mortgage business under a five-year lease, 
with the option to extend the lease by an additional five years. Therefore, decisions related to the 
reconfiguration and expansion of the Cheese Factory would not contribute to a change in the 
Property's value as a rental business. Moreover, decisions relating to the proposed project to expand 
and reconfigure the Cheese Factory for a multi-tenant marketplace featuring artisan foods, coffee, 
baked goods, and restaurants would not cause a prudent person to find that such decisions would 
contribute to a change in the mortgage business' value. 
Accordingly, you may take part in City Council decisions relating to the reconfiguration and 
expansion of the Cheese Factory: 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
By: 
SKP:jgl 
5 Where the source of income is a business entity, Regulation 18702.3{a)(4) refers back to the standard applied 
in 18702.1 for business entities. Therefore, we do not analyze Supervisor Adam's source of income interest separately.
